free_text: Experimental excitation functions of deuteron induced
reactions on natural thallium up to 50 MeV
Natural thallium targets (19.9 micron) were
prepared by  electrodeposition on thin copper foils
with nominal thickness 25  and 12.5 micron.
Samples were
prepared by electrodeposition onto cupper backing.
Irradiation was done using stacked target method.
Target thickness: 19.9 micron natural thallium target
backing: 12.5 and 25 micron cupper monitor foils: 27
micron aluminium foils energy degraders: 100 micron
lanthanum foils Bombarding deuteron energy: 50 MeV
beam intensity:110 nA irradiation time: 40min 3%
correction was applied to the measured beam intensity
using monitor reactions.
The initial uncertainty of the beam energy
was +-0.1MeV. Due to accumulating influence of the
variations in foil thickness and energy spread, the
uncertainty in the median energy estimation is
increasing along the stack, reaching a maximum of
4 MeV in the last foil.
The uncertainty of the cross-sections was
estimated from the independent relative errors of the
linearly contributing processes.
number of target nuclei and uniformity (5%)
beam intensity (5%)
detector efficiency (5%)
gamma-ray abundances  (1-5%)
statistical and peak fitting (1-5%)
The overall uncertainty is evaluated at 8-13%.
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes: TALYS1.4
The measured experimental data were compared to
those found in the literature.
Fitted excitation functions were used for
determination of integral physical yields.
